place: Rochester Institute Of Technology, New York

facts.text: RIT began when the Rochester Athenaeum, a literary society founded in 1829, merged with the Mechanics Institute, a technical school started in 1885.
In 1961, a $3.27 million donation from Grace Watson let RIT buy a 1,300‑acre campus south of Rochester along the Genesee River.
RIT became the first U.S. college to host the National Technical Institute for the Deaf in 1966, a pioneering program for deaf students.
The campus’s Field House, opened in 2004, was named after Lucius “Bob” Gordon and his wife Marie and hosts concerts, graduations, and sports.
In 2009, RIT earned the title “Campus Sustainability Leader” from the Sustainable Endowments Institute for its green initiatives.
The original downtown Rochester campus was bounded by the Erie Canal, South Plymouth Avenue, Spring Street, and South Washington Street.
